tweets.text:
Teens share 3x more than adults on social apps.

Not because they're addicted.

Because they still believe their voice matters.

Build for that belief.

A Kenyan freelancer just got paid $500 in USDC.

His bank wanted 5 days + $25 fees.

His wallet took 3 minutes + $0.50.

Which future do you think he's choosing?

130+ countries exploring CBDCs.

Only 3 have working retail systems.

The gap between announcement and reality is where most projects die.

Building > Announcing

Traditional bank: 0.5% interest, 3 days to withdraw.

DeFi protocol: 4% interest, 3 seconds to withdraw.

The risk? You're your own bank.

The reward? You're your own bank.

Web3's biggest problem isn't scalability.

It's explaining itself.

If you need 10 minutes to explain your product, you've already lost 99% of users.

Simplify or die.

Bitcoin at $60k isn't about Bitcoin.

It's about the dollar being questioned by a generation that watched their parents' savings evaporate.

Money is a belief system. Beliefs are shifting.

The best products don't explain what they do.

They make users feel something.

TBH made teens feel seen.
Gas made them feel popular.

Emotion > Function

$180B in stablecoins.

99% in USDC/USDT.

The 'decentralized' stablecoins? Most failed or are under $1B.

Users don't care about ideology. They care about what works.

We killed 14 apps before TBH went viral.

Each taught us one thing about user psychology.

The 15th combined them all.

Failure is data. Collect it.

China's e-CNY: 260 million wallets.
Nigeria's eNaira: 0.5% adoption.

Same technology. Different execution.

The 'how' matters more than the 'what'.
